classification: Champagne — Échelle des Crus, Grand Cru villages

provenance.authority: Échelle des Crus — the 17 historically-fixed Grand Cru (100%-rated) villages of Champagne
provenance.commune_footprints: IGN Admin Express commune polygons, fetched by the INSEE codes below (a subset of data/wine-map/champagne-communes.json — INAO 'Aires géographiques des AOC/AOP', Licence Ouverte)
caveats: A Champagne Grand Cru is a COMMUNE rating (the whole village is rated 100% on the Échelle des Crus), not a parcel appellation. The commune footprint is therefore an over-approximation of the actual rated vineyard area — acceptable at village display zoom, a documented approximation otherwise.

'Aÿ' is now inside the commune nouvelle 'Aÿ-Champagne' (INSEE 51030), which also contains Mareuil-sur-Aÿ (Premier Cru) and Bisseuil; the 51030 footprint over-includes those. Refining Aÿ to its historic déléguée footprint is a follow-up.
'Le Mesnil-sur-Oger' is the commune officially named 'Mesnil-sur-Oger' (INSEE 51367); the display name keeps the traditional 'Le Mesnil-sur-Oger'.

modeling_decision: Recommended: kind = SITE, is_appellation = false, appellation_level = null, primary_parent = france.champagne, display_tier = 2. is_appellation=false keeps them out of the AOC/appellation counts (a Grand Cru village is a rating, not an AOC) and avoids the classification-coupling constraint + the foundation 'appellations == aoc' assertion; the only test bumps are total/current places +17, tree +17. A later pass can add an Échelle-des-Crus designation + a tile 'level' hook for GC colouring.
